title: Cyclist magazine issue 174: on sale now
content:
Cyclist
Cyclist magazine issue 174: on sale now

Cyclist magazine Issue 174, May 2026, is on sale now. Subscribe to Cyclist or buy issue 174 here.

There are many attractions to riding a bike in a place like the Alps. There are the obvious ones – the grand vistas, the chance to take on epic climbs and sweeping descents, the food and culture… the list goes on.

But there are other less tangible riches to be had – the chance to follow in the wheeltracks of cycling legends, to hear the stories and local legends, to get a sense of the history and heritage of far-flung places, to breathe the local air.

Take our Big Ride in the May 2026 issue of Cyclist magazine, available now on newsstands or from the Cyclist Store. Our starting point is a train station in Chiusaforte in northern Italy that hasn’t seen a train pass through for three decades. Where once it served as a vital connection between Italy and Austria, today it’s a cycleway that runs all the way to Italy’s Adriatic coast.

But for our purposes it’s simply a jumping off point for a ride that first heads north up the Nassfeldpass into Austria before returning for a loop that delves deep into the untouched countryside in and around the Frulian Alps.

It’s certainly a ride with its challenges, but nothing along the lines of what riders face in The Accursed Race, a fully self-supported 1,600km ultra-endurance event in the Balkans that is so committed to sustainability that those taking part are required to reach the start line without taking a flight. We speak to four riders who took part in the 2025 edition to find out whether The Accursed Race was a curse or a blessing.

On the pro side we relive four of the biggest spring Classics – Strade Bianche, Milan-San Remo, the Tour of Flanders and Paris-Roubaix – through some of the best photos capturing crucial moments from these epic races, and hear first-hand from the riders themselves about their experiences in the peloton on the day.

It’s enough to leave you yearning for some serenity, and that’s precisely what our latest Classic Climb delivers – so much so that it’s actually called The Serenity Climb. It’s something of a hidden gem on Gran Canaria, itself a place that may not immediately spring to mind as a riding destination but could serve as a movie set purpose-built to advertise cycling. Read all about it in our May 2026 issue.

One man who has plenty of stories to tell from his pro career is George Hincapie, who raced 17 consecutive Tours de France and rode 50 Monuments but these days is fully focused on his new American pro team, Modern Adventure Pro Cycling. We speak to ‘Big George’ about his journey into cycling and how he’s bringing his considerable experience to bear in helping forge the careers of the next generation of US riders.

We haven’t forgotten about the bikes themselves, and this issue features a true icon as the subject of the latest instalment in our Dream Bikes series – the Festka Scalatore, which combines the best efforts of both robots and humans in one timeless package. Up front we feature Giant’s latest Propel, a bike that’s already become a firm favourite among Jayco-AlUla’s WorldTour pros, while our reviews section features another top-tier pro machine in the Colnago V5Rs alongside tests of the Open Upper 2.0 and Singular Kite Ti gravel bikes.

There’s plenty more besides in the May 2026 issue of Cyclist, so make sure to pick up your copy on newsstands or at the Cyclist Store or get a Cyclist magazine subscription to have every issue delivered free, straight to your door.

Right now is the perfect time to subscribe too, as our New Year’s Intro Offer gives you the chance to receive your first three issues for just £5, with no further obligation provided you cancel within the three-issue time period. Or if you’re looking for a great gift for someone else, why not treat them to a gift subscription, which includes not only the magazine but a range of goodies too all packaged up in a stylish presentation box.

Beyond that, there are three levels of Cyclist subscriptions available. For access to the Cyclist Rides online archive, prices start at £19.95 for six months. For the Cyclist digital package, which includes the digital version of Cyclist magazine plus Cyclist Rides, prices start at £21.95 for six months. For the full Cyclist membership, which includes Cyclist print magazine, the digital version of Cyclist, and Cyclist Rides, prices start at £37.95 for six months.

A one-year Cyclist membership costs £75.95 if you choose the direct debit option, which will automatically renew, or £87.95 without direct debit.

A Cyclist membership or digital subscription also currently come with 60 days of free Laka bike insurance, plus discounted tickets to Cyclist Track Days and complementary tickets to Cycling Electric Demo Days.

author: Martin James
